016package org.consensusj.bitcoinj.signing;
017
018import org.bitcoinj.base.Address;
019import org.bitcoinj.base.Coin;
020import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
021import java.util.List;
022import java.util.Map;
023import java.util.stream.Collectors;
024
025// A SigningRequest should be immutable *and* complete/ready-to-sign. There should be some kind of SigningRequestBuilder
026// to build a SigningRequest.
027/**
028 * A transaction signing request with immutable data specifying the transaction.
029 * <p>
030 * This is an experiment as to what immutable transactions might look like in <b>bitcoinj</b>.
031 * At some point in the future I would like to propose some refactoring in bitcoinj to implement
032 * immutable transactions in a mostly-compatible way with the existing transaction classes.
033 */
034public interface SigningRequest {
035    List<TransactionInputData> inputs();
036    List<TransactionOutputData> outputs();
037
038    static SigningRequest of(List<TransactionInputData> inputs, List<TransactionOutputData> outputs) {
039        return new DefaultSigningRequest(inputs, outputs);
040    }
041
042    static SigningRequest of(List<TransactionInputData> inputs, Map<Address, Coin> outputMap) {
043        List<TransactionOutputData> outs = outputMap.entrySet().stream()
044                .map(e -> new TransactionOutputAddress(e.getValue(), e.getKey()))
045                .collect(Collectors.toList());
046        return SigningRequest.of(inputs, outs);
047    }
048
049    /**
050     * bitcoinj signing (currently) uses mutable {@link Transaction} objects, this convenience method will create
051     * a completed, unsigned bitcoinj {@code Transaction} if you want to use bitcoinj to sign this request.
052     * @return an unsigned bitcoinj transaction
053     */
054    Transaction toUnsignedTransaction();
055}
